title: viola calcarata subsp. zoysii
intro: Viola calcarata subsp. zoysii is a subspecies of flowering plant, commonly known as the Alpine pansy, that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections. The plant is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being, and it is also used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. In herbal preparations, Viola calcarata subsp. zoysii is often combined with other plants to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to promote relaxation and support immune function.
